posts.author: Chetanpinto
posts.text: Any Manappuram shareholder would be truly delighted or atleast reassured to hear this. But this is also the GenAI era and GenAI is known for making up facts in terms of hallucinations and it’s important to be cautious about this as small investors. Therefore, best if there was a way we could also independently validate this turnaround accomplishment track record of Bain in India so that the value of holding this stock genuinely benefits it’s small shareholders Disc: Invested

posts.author: senthil.prabhuvp
posts.text: 1.The Successful “India” Legacy Bain Capital’s history in India isn’t just about banks; they have a track record of identifying structural shifts before they become mainstream. Hero MotoCorp (The Honda Exit): In 2011, Bain (along with GIC) stepped in to help the Munjal family buy out Honda’s 26% stake. The Result: This was a massive success. Bain helped bridge a critical financing gap during a messy corporate divorce. They exited 3–4 years later with a 2x return (doubling their money) and nearly ₹360 crore in dividends alone. Genpact (Scaling the Leader): In 2012, they bought a 30% stake for $…
